channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Analytics Engine API increased errors
item.description: Apr 22, 13:06 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 22, 13:01 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with the Analytics Engine API. Some customers may be seeing an increase of 5xx errors. We are working to mitigate the problem, more updates...

item.title: Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says
item.description: British businesses need to prepare themselves to defend against cyberattacks because the U.K. could be targeted “at scale,” if it became involved in an international conflict. The post Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says appeared first on...

item.title: New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention
item.description: Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the malware targets recovery mechanisms, overwrites drives, and systematically deletes files. The post New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: introducing the 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Introducing: 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now What actually matters in AI right now? It’s getting harder to tell amid the constant launches,...

item.title: Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers
item.description: The exploitation of the command injection vulnerability started one year after public disclosure and PoC exploit code publication. The post Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Struggle With SBOM Data
item.description: Researcher says the missing piece is a governance-driven intelligence layer that turns SBOM and VEX data into explainable security decisions. The post Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Struggle With SBOM Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the novel file wiper has been used in a destructive campaign targeting the energy and utilities sector...

item.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
item.description: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents. The more worrying part sat inside the private messages. Some of those...

item.title: AI needs a strong data fabric to deliver business value
item.description: Artificial intelligence is moving quickly in the enterprise, from experimentation to everyday use. Organizations are deploying copilots, agents, and predictive systems across finance, supply chains, human resources, and customer operations. By the end of 2025, half of companies used AI in at least...

item.title: 3 things Michelle Kim is into right now
item.description: Isegye Idol If you thought K-pop was weird, virtual idols—humans who perform as anime-style digital characters via motion capture—will blow your mind. My favorite is a girl group called Isegye Idol, created by Woowakgood, a Korean VTuber (a streamer who likewise performs as a digital persona)....

item.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carries a CVSS score of 9.1 out of 10.0. It's rated Important in severity. An anonymous researcher has...

item.title: Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU
item.description: The company released 481 new security patches across 28 product families, including over 300 fixes for remotely exploitable, unauthenticated flaws. The post Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Zone Activation Delays
item.description: Apr 22, 08:30 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is experiencing delays in activating customer sites. This does not impact existing active sites already in production. We are working to understand the full impact and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a dynamic DNS-based command-and-control server over HTTPS and supports remote shell access, file operations,...

item.title: Cohere AI Terrarium Sandbox Flaw Enables Root Code Execution, Container Escape
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated 9.3 on the CVSS scoring system. "Sandbox escape vulnerability in Terrarium allows arbitrary code...

item.title: Tencent Launches Overseas Beta of QClaw AI Agent Platform
item.description: Tencent launched the overseas beta of its AI agent platform QClaw, offering a no-install, multi-model tool for global users with built-in security monitoring.

item.title: Yixing Intelligence Raises RMB 1.5 Billion in Series B for RISC-V AI Chips
item.description: Chinese AI chip startup Yixing Intelligence secured RMB 1.5 billion (about $210 million) in Series B funding to advance its RISC-V-based AI computing platform.

item.title: Lingji Tianci Secures Multi-Million Yuan Funding for AI-Powered Children’s Toy
item.description: Beijing-based Lingji Tianci raised tens of millions of yuan (several million USD) to develop “Jollybubu,” an AI-powered children’s toy system combining physical dolls with multi-agent interaction.

item.title: Alibaba Introduces “Qwen Dimples” Avatar to Unify AI Assistant Ecosystem
item.description: Alibaba launched “Qwen Dimples,” a unified AI assistant avatar integrated across its ecosystem, as the Qwen app reached 166 million monthly active users in China.

item.title: On Solving the Multiple Variable Gapped Longest Common Subsequence Problem
item.description: arXiv:2604.18645v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper addresses the Variable Gapped Longest Common Subsequence (VGLCS) problem, a generalization of the classical LCS problem involving flexible gap constraints between consecutive solutions' characters. The problem arises in molecular sequence...

item.title: Beyond One Output: Visualizing and Comparing Distributions of Language Model Generations
item.description: arXiv:2604.18724v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Users typically interact with and evaluate language models via single outputs, but each output is just one sample from a broad distribution of possible completions. This interaction hides distributional structure such as modes, uncommon edge cases,...

item.title: ARES: Adaptive Red-Teaming and End-to-End Repair of Policy-Reward System
item.description: arXiv:2604.18789v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) is central to aligning Large Language Models (LLMs), yet it introduces a critical vulnerability: an imperfect Reward Model (RM) can become a single point of failure when it fails to penalize unsafe...

item.title: AI scientists produce results without reasoning scientifically
item.description: arXiv:2604.18805v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based systems are increasingly deployed to conduct scientific research autonomously, yet whether their reasoning adheres to the epistemic norms that make scientific inquiry self-correcting is poorly understood. Here, we...

item.title: Quantum inspired qubit qutrit neural networks for real time financial forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2604.18838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This research investigates the performance and efficacy of machine learning models in stock prediction, comparing Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs), Quantum Qubit-based Neural Networks (QQBNs), and Quantum Qutrit-based Neural Networks (QQTNs). By...

item.title: Compile to Compress: Boosting Formal Theorem Provers by Compiler Outputs
item.description: arXiv:2604.18587v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant potential in formal theorem proving, yet state-of-the-art performance often necessitates prohibitive test-time compute via massive roll-outs or extended context windows. In this work, we...

item.title: Easy Samples Are All You Need: Self-Evolving LLMs via Data-Efficient Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.18639v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Previous LLMs-based RL studies typically follow either supervised learning with high annotation costs, or unsupervised paradigms using voting or entropy-based rewards. However, their performance remains far from satisfactory due to the substantial...

item.title: FASE : A Fairness-Aware Spatiotemporal Event Graph Framework for Predictive Policing
item.description: arXiv:2604.18644v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predictive policing systems that allocate patrol resources based solely on predicted crime risk can unintentionally amplify racial disparities through feedback driven data bias. We present FASE, a Fairness Aware Spatiotemporal Event Graph framework,...

item.title: Curiosity-Critic: Cumulative Prediction Error Improvement as a Tractable Intrinsic Reward for World Model Training
item.description: arXiv:2604.18701v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Local prediction-error-based curiosity rewards focus on the current transition without considering the world model's cumulative prediction error across all visited transitions. We introduce Curiosity-Critic, which grounds its intrinsic reward in the...

item.title: The Cost of Relaxation: Evaluating the Error in Convex Neural Network Verification
item.description: arXiv:2604.18728v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many neural network (NN) verification systems represent the network's input-output relation as a constraint program. Sound and complete, representations involve integer constraints, for simulating the activations. Recent works convexly relax the...

item.title: Two-dimensional early exit optimisation of LLM inference
item.description: arXiv:2604.18592v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce a two-dimensional (2D) early exit strategy that coordinates layer-wise and sentence-wise exiting for classification tasks in large language models. By processing input incrementally sentence-by-sentence while progressively activating...

item.title: Probing for Reading Times
item.description: arXiv:2604.18712v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Probing has shown that language model representations encode rich linguistic information, but it remains unclear whether they also capture cognitive signals about human processing. In this work, we probe language model representations for human...

item.title: Characterizing AlphaEarth Embedding Geometry for Agentic Environmental Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.18715v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Earth observation foundation models encode land surface information into dense embedding vectors, yet the geometric structure of these representations and its implications for downstream reasoning remain underexplored. We characterize the manifold...

item.title: Scripts Through Time: A Survey of the Evolving Role of Transliteration in NLP
item.description: arXiv:2604.18722v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-lingual transfer in NLP is often hindered by the ``script barrier'' where differences in writing systems inhibit transfer learning between languages. Transliteration, the process of converting the script, has emerged as a powerful technique to...

item.title: Investigating Counterfactual Unfairness in LLMs towards Identities through Humor
item.description: arXiv:2604.18729v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humor holds up a mirror to social perception: what we find funny often reflects who we are and how we judge others. When language models engage with humor, their reactions expose the social assumptions they have internalized from training data. In...

item.title: OnePlus Ace 6 Ultra Unveils New Colorway, Boosts Gaming and Design
item.description: OnePlus previewed the Ace 6 Ultra with a new “Metal Storm” design and advanced gaming features, including 165fps performance powered by MediaTek Dimensity 9500.

item.title: ChatGPT free users are seeing errors when having conversations
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Conversations (Partial outage)

item.title: [AINews] OpenAI launches GPT-Image-2
item.description: with Cursor getting a $10B contract with xAI and a right to acquire for $60B.

item.title: Roundtables: Unveiling The 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Watch a special edition of Roundtables simulcast live from EmTech AI, MIT Technology Review’s signature conference for AI leadership. Subscribers got an exclusive first look at a new list capturing 10 key technologies, emerging trends, bold ideas, and powerful...

item.title: The new word in home construction could be “plastics”
item.description: Single-use plastics are a persistent source of environmental pollution, and the need to house a growing global population puts increasing pressure on resources such as timber. MIT engineers have an idea that could make a dent in both problems at once. In a recent study, a team led by mechanical...

item.title: Page Rules with Host Header Override issues
item.description: Apr 21, 20:19 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 21, 19:57 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 21, 18:09 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare has identified an issue with customers creating/updating Page Rules with the Host Header Override setting....

item.title: Cloudflare Gateway HTTP Logs issues
item.description: Apr 21, 19:36 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is observing issues with Cloudflare Gateway HTTP Logs service that may delay log ingestion.

item.title: Investigating potential impact to Realtime services in Bombay
item.description: Apr 21, 18:37 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 21, 18:30 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 21, 17:46 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating reports of degraded Realtime service in the Mumbai...

item.title: Benign Overfitting in Adversarial Training for Vision Transformers
item.description: Despite the remarkable success of Vision Transformers (ViTs) across a wide range of vision tasks, recent studies have revealed that they remain vulnerable to adversarial examples, much like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). A common empirical defense strategy is adversarial training, yet the...

item.title: Adaptive MSD-Splitting: Enhancing C4.5 and Random Forests for Skewed Continuous Attributes
item.description: The discretization of continuous numerical attributes remains a persistent computational bottleneck in the induction of decision trees, particularly as dataset dimensions scale. Building upon the recently proposed MSD-Splitting technique -- which bins continuous data using the empirical mean and...

item.title: Disruption with projects service
item.description: Apr 21, 16:45 UTCUpdate - The mitigation is deployed and we are seeing recorvery in the queues and will provide an update as to when full recovery will be realized.Apr 21, 16:18 UTCUpdate - We are deploying a fix to relieve the queue of delayed data. Some users may still experience delays with...

item.title: EgoSelf: From Memory to Personalized Egocentric Assistant
item.description: Egocentric assistants often rely on first-person view data to capture user behavior and context for personalized services. Since different users exhibit distinct habits, preferences, and routines, such personalization is essential for truly effective assistance. However, effectively integrating...

item.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
item.description: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to bring the power of frontier AI to organizations around the world.

item.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
item.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to...

item.title: New cross domain guidance for government, industry and the wider security community
item.description: Ensuring cross domain technologies are better understood - and more easily deployed - across sectors.

item.title: Cyber chief: UK faces "perfect storm" for cyber security
item.description: As the technology landscape develops, the definition of cyber security is expanding with it.
